input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A man wearing glasses and a blue shirt with an orange graphic stands centrally against a blue geometric background. He gestures with both hands, shifting them slightly closer together while speaking. White text on the right reads "Proudly brought to you by DOMAIN.COM" next to a red circular logo with a white D.</think><answer></answer>

compressed_segments.text:
[0-4] A man wearing glasses and a blue shirt with an orange graphic stands centrally against a blue geometric background, gesturing. White text on the right reads "Proudly brought to you by DOMAIN.COM" next to a red circular logo with a white D. He shifts hands to a halting motion, palms forward, then smiles.
[4-8] The red logo and sponsor text vanish as the camera zooms in. A white geometric line graphic appears on the right, resolving to display "Trace Dominguez" and "@tracedominguez" in white text next to a left-pointing arrow.
[8-14] The name graphic disappears, and the view widens to reveal the man's hands and torso. He remains centered, gesturing with open palms and turning his head left while speaking against the blue network background. No text overlays are visible.

[20-22] The scene cuts to a static medical photograph of a human torso with dark skin. White arrows point to the nipples and several spots aligned vertically down the abdomen. Small white text in the bottom left corner reads "Indian Journal of Human Genetics."
[22-28] The view returns to the man against the blue background. He initially looks slightly right with hands lowered, then raises his hands to waist level with palms facing inward, widening his eyes and opening his mouth as he gestures slightly outward.

A man in a blue t-shirt with an orange graphic and clear glasses speaks and gestures with hands at waist level [28-30]. The camera zooms in to a medium close-up, cropping his hands, as he continues talking and turning his head slightly [30-34]. He then shifts to the right side of the frame while a white silhouette graphic appears on the left, displaying red vertical lines running down the torso from the armpit area [34-38]. The graphic disappears, and the man moves back to the center [38-40]. Finally, he raises both hands to waist level again, gesturing with open palms while speaking directly forward [40-42].

[42-46] The man in the blue t-shirt with the orange graphic remains centered against a blue geometric background. He lowers his hands from waist-level to his sides, shifting from speaking to a neutral look with pressed lips. He keeps his hands lowered near his waist and opens his mouth to begin speaking again.
[46-56] He raises his hands from lowered to gesture outward at waist level, keeping palms facing inward. Throughout this duration, he continues speaking with an open mouth and animated facial expression. He maintains his central position in the frame, glasses and beard visible. The lighting and background pattern remain constant. No text or graphical overlays appear.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

White text on the right reads "Proudly brought to you by DOMAIN.COM" next to a red circular logo with a white D. He shifts hands to a halting motion, palms forward, then smiles.
[4-8] The red logo and sponsor text vanish as the camera zooms in. A white geometric line graphic appears on the right, resolving to display "Trace Dominguez" and "@tracedominguez" in white text next to a left-pointing arrow.
[8-14] The name graphic disappears, and the view widens to reveal the man's hands and torso. He remains centered, gesturing with open palms and turning his head left while speaking against the blue network background. No text overlays are visible.

[14-20] A man wearing a blue t-shirt with an orange graphic and clear glasses stands against a blue geometric network background. He lowers his hands from a mid-chest gesture to a clasped position near his waist, then raises his right hand to point his index finger upward while speaking.
[20-22] The scene cuts to a static medical photograph of a human torso with dark skin. White arrows point to the nipples and several spots aligned vertically down the abdomen. Small white text in the bottom left corner reads "Indian Journal of Human Genetics."
[22-28] The view returns to the man against the blue background. He initially looks slightly right with hands lowered, then raises his hands to waist level with palms facing inward, widening his eyes and opening his mouth as he gestures slightly outward.

recent_thinks: [28-30] The man in the blue t-shirt with the orange graphic continues speaking with an open mouth. He gestures with both hands held at waist level, palms facing inward and slightly upward. His facial expression remains animated with wide eyes behind clear glasses against the blue geometric network background.

[30-32] The camera zooms in to a closer shot of the man wearing a blue t-shirt with an orange graphic and clear glasses. He looks directly forward with his mouth open as if speaking. The blue geometric network background remains visible, but his hands are now cropped out of the frame.

[32-34] The man in the blue t-shirt with the orange graphic continues speaking against the blue geometric background. He turns his head slightly from left to right while maintaining an open-mouthed expression. The camera remains in a medium close-up shot, keeping his hands out of frame. No new text or graphical overlays appear during this segment.

[34-36] A man in a blue shirt with an orange graphic and glasses stands on the right side of the frame against a blue network background. To his left, a white silhouette of a human figure wearing dark shorts appears. Red lines are drawn vertically down the torso of the silhouette from the armpit area.

[36-38] The man in the blue shirt and clear glasses continues speaking on the right side of the frame. Beside him, the white silhouette graphic with red vertical lines on the torso remains visible and static. The man's mouth moves as he talks, and he maintains eye contact with the camera against the blue geometric background.

samples.output: <think>The white silhouette graphic featuring red vertical lines vanishes from the left side of the screen. The man in the blue t-shirt shifts position from the right to the center of the frame, now facing forward directly against the blue geometric network background.</think><answer></answer>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

White text on the right reads "Proudly brought to you by DOMAIN.COM" next to a red circular logo with a white D. He shifts hands to a halting motion, palms forward, then smiles.
[4-8] The red logo and sponsor text vanish as the camera zooms in. A white geometric line graphic appears on the right, resolving to display "Trace Dominguez" and "@tracedominguez" in white text next to a left-pointing arrow.
[8-14] The name graphic disappears, and the view widens to reveal the man's hands and torso. He remains centered, gesturing with open palms and turning his head left while speaking against the blue network background. No text overlays are visible.

[14-20] A man wearing a blue t-shirt with an orange graphic and clear glasses stands against a blue geometric network background. He lowers his hands from a mid-chest gesture to a clasped position near his waist, then raises his right hand to point his index finger upward while speaking.
[20-22] The scene cuts to a static medical photograph of a human torso with dark skin. White arrows point to the nipples and several spots aligned vertically down the abdomen. Small white text in the bottom left corner reads "Indian Journal of Human Genetics."
[22-28] The view returns to the man against the blue background. He initially looks slightly right with hands lowered, then raises his hands to waist level with palms facing inward, widening his eyes and opening his mouth as he gestures slightly outward.

A man in a blue t-shirt with an orange graphic and clear glasses speaks and gestures with hands at waist level [28-30]. The camera zooms in to a medium close-up, cropping his hands, as he continues talking and turning his head slightly [30-34]. He then shifts to the right side of the frame while a white silhouette graphic appears on the left, displaying red vertical lines running down the torso from the armpit area [34-38]. The graphic disappears, and the man moves back to the center [38-40]. Finally, he raises both hands to waist level again, gesturing with open palms while speaking directly forward [40-42].

recent_thinks: [42-44] The man in the blue t-shirt with the orange graphic remains centered against the blue geometric background. He lowers his hands from a raised waist-level position down to his sides. His facial expression shifts from speaking with an open mouth to a neutral expression with lips pressed together. No new text or graphical overlays appear.

[44-46] The man in the blue shirt with the orange graphic remains centered against the blue network background. His hands stay lowered near his waist. His mouth opens as he begins speaking again, transitioning from a closed-mouth expression. The lighting and background pattern remain constant throughout the sequence.

[46-48] The man in the blue t-shirt with the orange graphic raises his hands from a lowered position to gesture outward at waist level. He continues speaking with his mouth open against the blue geometric network background. His glasses and beard remain visible as he maintains a central position in the frame throughout the sequence.

[48-50] The man in the blue t-shirt with the orange graphic continues standing centrally against the blue geometric background. He maintains hand gestures at waist level with palms facing inward. His mouth remains open as he speaks, and his facial expression stays animated. No new text or graphical overlays appear during this interval.

queries.question: What entities are present and what are they doing?

answers.text: A man with clear glasses and a blue t-shirt featuring an orange graphic stands against a blue geometric network background. He speaks directly to the camera with an animated expression, often keeping his mouth open. Throughout the video, he gestures with both hands at waist level, palms facing inward or upward. At one point, a white human silhouette with red vertical lines appears beside him before vanishing as he shifts from the right side back to the center of the frame.

samples.output: <think>The man in the blue t-shirt with the orange graphic stands centrally against the blue geometric background. He continues gesturing with both hands raised at waist level, palms facing inward. His mouth remains open as he speaks, and his facial expression stays animated without new overlays.</think><answer></answer>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
